reason: The passage depicts Moses, the Hebrew leader, displaying humble characteristics consistent with biblical humility. He cries out to God in prayer (וַיִּצְעַק) rather than relying on his own authority, directs the people to trust in God's deliverance (הִֽתְיַצְב֗וּ וּרְאוּ֙ אֶת־ יְשׁוּעַ֣ת יְהוָ֔ה - 'stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD'), and states 'the LORD shall fight for you' rather than claiming credit for himself. He functions as God's instrument and intermediary, immediately obeying divine commands (lifting his staff over the sea) without hesitation or question. Throughout the passage, Moses attributes all power and deliverance to God, not to his own leadership abilities, which exemplifies biblical humility—the recognition of God's supremacy and submission to His will.
